title: Cancer couldn’t stop her. Now she’s running the Boston Marathon again.
author: Annie Jonas
bullets: Stephanie McHugh, 42, from Braintree, is participating in the 2026 Boston Marathon for the New England Patriots Foundation.
After overcoming Stage 3 breast cancer, McHugh returned to running with guidance from mentor Susan Hurley.
McHugh's marathon is dedicated to friends, mentor Susan, and proving resilience against cancer.

title: Markey presses Meta on facial recognition risks in smart glasses
author: Morgan Rousseau
bullets: Sen. Ed Markey and other senators express concerns over Meta's smart glasses with facial recognition capabilities.
Lawmakers warn the technology could discourage public dissent and infringe on privacy at political rallies.
Meta is asked to clarify data collection and security measures for potential biometric data by April 6.

title: Debate in Quincy over funding for new firefighting gear after current gear tests positive for PFAS
author: Morgan Rousseau
bullets: Quincy City Council delayed a $2.65 million vote to replace firefighter gear containing PFAS chemicals.
PFAS, found in current gear, poses cancer risks; firefighters call for urgent gear replacement.
Councilors seek more time to assess costs and durability of proposed PFAS-free gear.

title: The battle over the aging Attleboro Zoo is getting wild
author: Morgan Rousseau
bullets: Attleboro proposes converting Capron Park Zoo into a nature reserve to address $57 million in needed repairs.
The zoo's transformation aims to cut costs and focus on native wildlife conservation with potential for free admission.
Zoo staff express concern over the proposal, fearing it may lead to the eventual closure of the facility.

title: What does it mean to have a partner?
author: Autumn Sloboda
bullets: Colin and Abigail Wong of Watertown held a last-minute microwedding in Somerville due to a blizzard on January 26, 2026.
FX's "Love Story" about John F. Kennedy Jr. and Carolyn Bessette explores 1990s communication nostalgia, highlighted by Kara Baskin.
Discussion on the use of the term 'partner' in early relationships questions if it's interchangeable with 'boyfriend' among young adults.

title: After loss and cancer, a Burlington runner keeps moving toward Boston
author: Autumn Sloboda
bullets: Adam Averback, 44, of Burlington, MA, will run the 2026 Boston Marathon with Team MGH to honor family struggles.
Averback began running after his father's 2020 death from glioblastoma and his own 2021 testicular cancer diagnosis.
Despite a serious blood infection, Averback continued training, embodying resilience and commitment to honor his and his father's journey.

title: New Patriots S Kevin Byard working out with rumored New England trade target A.J. Brown
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: Patriots safety Kevin Byard and WR A.J. Brown are working out together, fueling trade rumors.
The Eagles declined a recent trade offer from the Patriots for wide receiver A.J. Brown.
Philadelphia may wait until June 1 to trade Brown to manage salary cap considerations.

title: James Hagens watch begins as Bruins brace for top prospect’s potential arrival
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: James Hagens completed his sophomore season at Boston College with 47 points in 34 games, signaling potential for the Bruins.
Boston Bruins may sign Hagens soon, offering a boost for the team's final regular-season stretch, says GM Don Sweeney.
Hagens' versatility as a forward provides valuable options for the Bruins' lineup, including potential roles on the wing.

title: 5 must-watch movies & TV shows streaming right now
author: Kevin Slane
bullets: 'Peaky Blinders: The Immortal Man' set in 1940 Birmingham is now streaming on Netflix.
Bradley Cooper's 'Is This Thing On?' starring Will Arnett is available on Disney+.
'Jury Duty: Company Retreat', a hidden camera comedy, is streaming on Prime Video.

title: ‘We’ll see’: Alex Cora mum on future with Red Sox
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: Alex Cora remains undecided on his future with the Boston Red Sox after his contract ends in 2027.
Cora's current contract, signed in July 2024, pays him over $7 million per season.
Cora needs 134 more wins to surpass Terry Francona for second-most managerial wins in Red Sox history.
